evals.prompt: Our site has 34% Chrome traffic, 12% Safari on macOS, 15% Chrome on Android, and 14% Safari on iOS. Design a browser matrix with tier assignments based on traffic share.
expected_patterns: mobile-chrome OR mobile-safari OR Android OR iOS

evals.prompt: Configure playwright.config.ts with P0 and P1 browsers: Chrome and Safari desktop, Chrome and Safari mobile, Firefox desktop.
anti_patterns: hardcoded viewport sizes instead of devices
mixing cloud platform config with local devices without clear intent

evals.prompt: Set up a BrowserStack Playwright config to test Chrome and Safari on Windows 11.
anti_patterns: hardcoded credentials instead of env variables

evals.prompt: Write a cross-browser test for a product grid layout that uses flexbox gap. Verify the grid displays 3 columns and has consistent spacing across Chrome, Safari, and Firefox.
anti_patterns: testing CSS internal properties instead of layout outcome
browserName check for flexbox gap (not needed in 2026)

evals.prompt: Write a test for a date input field that works across browsers. Handle the fact that Safari renders it differently than Chrome and Firefox.
anti_patterns: testing browser UI instead of functionality

evals.prompt: Write a test that verifies a copy-to-clipboard button works across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. Handle clipboard permission differences.
anti_patterns: no permission handling for different browsers
reading clipboard directly instead of verifying UI feedback

evals.prompt: Run the test suite on only P0 browsers (Chrome and Safari) for a PR check, and all browsers nightly. Show the CLI commands.
anti_patterns: running all browsers on every PR
no differentiation between PR and nightly CI

evals.prompt: Document three known cross-browser issues in your test suite: (1) scroll-behavior: smooth differences, (2) backdrop-filter not supported in older Firefox, (3) View Transitions API in Chrome vs Firefox.
expected_patterns: test verifies functional outcome not CSS
anti_patterns: testing pixel rendering instead of user task

evals.prompt: Set up a visual regression test for a homepage that generates browser-specific baselines across Chrome, Safari, and Firefox. Use Playwright screenshot comparison with pixel tolerance.
Test progressive enhancement: verify a contact form submits successfully even with JavaScript disabled in Chromium. Show the route interception.
anti_patterns: testing JS-disabled on non-Chromium browsers (not supported)
assuming form works without testing the outcome
